title: PPHC Expands MultiState Associates' Stakeholder Engagement Capabilities
content:
WASHINGTON, May  01, 2026  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Public Policy Holding Company, Inc. ("PPHC" or "the Company") (NASDAQ: PPHC), a leading global strategic communications provider, today announced that MultiState, a PPHC subsidiary providing state-level government relations, has hired Lee Cowen and Nicholas Evans, specialists in federal, state, and local government relations, through the acquisition of the assets of their respective management companies Cowen Consulting, LLC and Putnam Strategies, LLC.

The acqui-hire additions significantly expand MultiState's stakeholder engagement practice, which works with political organizations including governors' associations (NGA, RGA, DGA), attorney general groups (NAAG, RAGA, DAGA), and other state-level (political and policy) organizations. With the addition of Lee Cowen and Nicholas Evans, MultiState's dedicated stakeholder engagement team continues its growth, complementing a broader group of MultiState staff who engage in stakeholder work across their portfolios.

MultiState established its stakeholder engagement practice over the past three years under the leadership of Maggie Mick, who previously served as Chief Advancement Officer at the Council of State Governments. The hiring of Lee Cowen and Nicholas Evans accelerates the growth of the practice and positions the firm to better serve large clients who require sophisticated multistate strategies.

Lee Cowen and Nicholas Evans bring an established client roster of major national companies and organizations. They have built trusted relationships over decades in state government affairs, and their clients represent strong candidates for MultiState's broader suite of services, including issue management and lobbying compliance.

Stewart Hall, CEO of PPHC, commented:

“MultiState has built something unique and distinctive in the state government affairs market, and stakeholder engagement is a natural extension of that offering. Lee and Nicholas are highly experienced and well-regarded professionals with strong client relationships, and we see real opportunity to offer those clients the full breadth of MultiState’s and PPHC’s capabilities.”

Joe Crosby, CEO of MultiState Associates, commented:

“Lee and Nicholas are exactly the kind of people anyone would want on their team. They have been incredibly successful on their own yet recognize the additional value we collectively will bring to our clients. We’ve made significant investments in building our Stakeholder Engagement practice, guided by Maggie Mick and supported by our deeply experienced team — making this the perfect time for Lee and Nicholas to join us. Several of us have known Lee and Nicholas for decades; they are genuinely good people and extraordinary professionals who will accelerate our growth.”

Lee Cowen, Founder of Cowen Consulting, commented:

“Nicholas and I are thrilled to be joining MultiState and the entire PPHC family. We have known the MultiState folks for decades, and we're excited to be able to enhance their stakeholder engagement practice while also being able to offer our current - and future - clients more of the complimentary services provided by MultiState and the PPHC team. With increased focus on the state policy front, we became convinced that a more comprehensive suite of services - such as issues management, compliance, research, etc. - is necessary for businesses and associations as they work with state governments to achieve their policy goals. For Nicholas and me, MultiState was the obvious choice when looking for the right teammates.”

About MultiState Associates

MultiState Associates is a PPHC subsidiary providing state-level government relations, issue management, regulatory tracking, and lobbying compliance services. MultiState helps companies and trade associations navigate the multistate policy landscape through a combination of strategic advisory, technology-enabled monitoring, and direct stakeholder engagement.

About PPHC

Incorporated in 2014, PPHC is a global strategic communications platform that supports clients in enhancing and defending their reputations, advancing policy objectives, managing regulatory risk, and engaging with federal and state-level policymakers, stakeholders, media, and the public.

Engaged by approximately 1,400 clients, including companies, trade associations and non-governmental organizations, PPHC is active in all major sectors of the economy, including healthcare and pharmaceuticals, financial services, energy, technology, telecoms and transportation.

With operations across 18 offices in the United States and internationally, PPHC's services include government relations, public affairs and corporate communications, research and analytics, digital advocacy campaigning, and compliance support. The Company's shares are admitted to trading on the Nasdaq Global Market and on AIM, a market operated by the London Stock Exchange, under the ticker symbol "PPHC".

For more information, visit www.pphcompany.com.

title: Alphabet, Nvidia upgraded: Wall Street's top analyst calls
content:
The most talked about and market moving research calls around Wall Street are now in one place. Here are today's research calls that investors need to know, as compiled by The Fly.

Top 5 Upgrades:

Wells Fargo upgraded Alphabet (GOOGL) to Overweight from Equal Weight with a price target of $387, up from $354. The company has a leadership position in the "three key traits" of an AI winner, namely customer data, distribution, and compute capacity, the firm tells investors in a research note. Aletheia upgraded Nvidia (NVDA) to Buy from Hold with a $250 price target. The firm views the stock as "too cheap to ignore" into Nvidia's Q4 report, which it expects to top expectations. Lake Street upgraded Tandem Diabetes(TNDM) to Buy from Hold with a price target of $50, up from $14. The firm believes Tandem's transition to a pharmacy model will result in more predictable growth, improved margins, and meaningful free cash flow. UBS upgraded BlackRock (BLK) to Buy from Neutral with a $1,280 price target. The upgrade is supported by strong Q4 results, positive commentary at the UBS Financials conference, and a solid start to Q1 flows, the firm tells investors in a research note. Baird upgraded Henry Schein(HSIC) to Outperform from Neutral with a price target of $100, up from $78. The firm believes the bias across its dental names remains to the upside.

Top 5 Downgrades:

Jefferies downgraded Workday (WDAY) to Hold from Buy with a price target of $150, down from $325. The firm says the apps software names have been hit harder than overall software. Jefferies also downgraded DocuSign (DOCU) and Monday.com (MNDY) to Hold from Buy. BofA downgraded General Mills(GIS) to Neutral from Buy with a price target of $48, down from $55, following the CAGNY conference. The firm's prior Buy thesis was predicated on a re-acceleration in Pet and stabilization in North America Retail, but "that has gone stale," it tells investors. Baird downgraded Arcellx (ACLX) to Neutral from Outperform with a price target of $115, up from $106, after Gilead Sciences (GILD) announced that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire the company for $115 per share in cash at closing and one contingent value right of $5 per share, which represents an implied equity value of $7.8B payable at closing. Wells Fargo, Truist, and Canaccord also downgraded the stock to Neutral-equivalent ratings. JPMorgan downgraded VF Corp.(VFC) to Underweight from Neutral with a price target of $18, down from $19. The firm downgraded the shares after reducing its fecal 2027 and 2028 earnings estimates below consensus. Jefferies downgraded Deere (DE) to Underperform from Hold with a price target of $550, up from $475. The firm says that while the agriculture cycle should trough this year, a recovery will require an improvement in U.S. farmer incomes with crop prices the key driver.

Story Continues

Top 5 Initiations:

Citi initiated coverage of York Space Systems(YSS) with a Buy rating and $37 price target. The stock offers a "high risk/high reward play on the space megatrend," the firm tells investors in a research note. Canaccord, JPMorgan, Wells Fargo, Baird, Goldman Sachs, Jefferies, Raymond James, and Needham also started coverage of the stock with Buy-equivalent ratings, while Truist initiated with a Hold. Oppenheimer initiated coverage of Public Policy Holding Co(PPHC) with an Outperform rating and $16 price target. The firm notes the company is expanding to state and international lobbying, while simultaneously building out a complementary strategic communications practice. Canaccord also started coverage of the stock with Buy Citi initiated coverage of PicPay (PICS) with a Buy rating and $28 price target, implying 85% upside. The firm sees significant earnings growth for the company from credit-related activity. Mizuho and RBC Capital also started coverage of the stock with Buy-equivalent ratings. JPMorgan initiated coverage of Ethos (LIFE) with an Overweight rating and $13 price target. JPMorgan thinks Ethos has room to grow, specifically through agency. Citi, Goldman Sachs, BofA, Baird, Citizens, William Blair, and Barclays also started coverage of the stock with Buy-equivalent ratings. DA Davidson initiated coverage of Bob's Discount Furniture(BOBS) with a Buy rating and $26 price target. The firm says the company is operating in a "large and fragmented industry on the verge of recovery."

View Comments

title: Catalyst Watch: Nvidia blockbuster, JPMorgan event, and reactions to the tariff reset
content:
[New York City and London multiple exposure image]

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - FEBRUARY 23

 	* TARIFF WATCH - Analysts and investors will be busy resetting expectations on nearly every sector after the Supreme Court ruling on President Trump's sweeping tariff policies. 
 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - AXT (AXTI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXTI]) and CoreWeave (CRWV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWV]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Sensei Bio (SNSE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNSE]), Calumet (CLMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLMT]), and Energy Services of America (ESOA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ESOA]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Virtuix (VTIX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTIX]), SPS Commerce (SPSC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPSC]), and SimilarWeb (SMWB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SMWB]).
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Dominion Energy (D [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D]), Diamondback Energy (FANG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FANG]), and Domino's Pizza (DPZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DPZ]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BBBY]) and Vir Biotechnology (VIR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VIR]) after they report.
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Marriott International (MAR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAR]), Delta Air Lines (DAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DAL]), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JNJ]), and Northrop Grumman (NOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NOC]).
 	* IPO WATCH - The quiet period ends on York Space Systems (YSS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YSS]), PicS (PICS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PICS]), Ethos Technologies (LIFE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LIFE]), and Public Policy Holding (PPHC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PPHC]) to free up analysts to post ratings. The IPO lockup period expires on blocks of shares of Picard Medical (PMI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PMI]).
 	* ALL DAY - Fed Governor Lisa Cook will participate in a panel on “Artificial Intelligence and Productivity” at the NABE Annual Economic Policy Conference in Washington, D.C. 
 	* 8:25 A.M. JPMorgan (JPM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JPM]) will host a company update in New York City. Analysts expect the firm-wide overview by CFO Jeremy Barnum to be more in-depth than in recent years, as it will likely include key points for each main business line. CEO Jamie Dimon is anticipated to close out the event with prepared remarks and Q&A.

TUESDAY - FEBRUARY 24

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Home Depot (HD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HD]), MercadoLibre (MELI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MELI]), American Tower (AMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMT]), Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KDP]), and Workday (WDAY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDAY]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for AMC Entertainment (AMC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMC]) and Revolve Group (RVLV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RVLV]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Apple (AAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AAPL]) will hold its annual meeting. Shareholders will vote on a proposal from the National Center for Public Policy Research requesting a board-level report on risks and costs of Apple’s reliance on and operations in China (supply chain, tariffs, rare earths, and security/espionage concerns). A vote on director re-election will include chair Art Levinson and director Ron Sugar, with the board explicitly waiving its usual age-75 guideline for those two.
 	* 9:00 A.M. The S&P/Case-Shiller House Price Index will be released.
 	* 10:00 A.M. The Conference Board will release its latest consumer confidence report.

WEDNESDAY - FEBRUARY 25

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Nvidia (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]), Salesforce (CRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM]), Lowe's (LOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LOW]), TJX Companies (TJX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TJX]), and Snowflake (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Redwire (RDW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDW]) and c3.ai (AI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AI]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Samsung's (SSNLF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SSNLF]) Galaxy Unpacked media event will include the unveiling of the new Galaxy S26 phone lineup, showcasing of new Galaxy AI features, and potentially new Galaxy Buds.
 	* ALL DAY - Lumen Technologies (LUMN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LUMN]) will hold its Investor Day. Management will lay out the next phase of the company's transformation, including product pipeline, commercial strategy, financial framework, and multiyear growth and earnings plans.
 	* ALL DAY - Intapp (INTA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTA]) will host an Investor Day in New York, where management is expected to highlight its AI‑driven vertical SaaS platform, role in legal/advisory/capital markets workflows, and key growth and financial metrics for the business.
 	* 8:00 A.M. Nasdaq (NDAQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NDAQ]) will hold its 2026 Investor Day at its Times Square headquarters (also webcast) from 8:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. ET, focusing on strategy, business mix, and long‑term financial objectives for the group.
 	* 8:30 A.M. It is the FDA action date for Eton Pharmaceuticals' (ETON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ETON]) ET‑600 desmopressin oral solution, which is being reviewed as a treatment for pediatric central diabetes insipidus/arginine vasopressin deficiency.
 	* 10:00 A.M. The New Home Sales report will be released by the Census Bureau.
 	* 5:00 P.M. Nvidia (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]) will hold its earnings conference call. Analysts expect crucial color on 2026–2027 AI capex plans from hyperscalers Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]), Meta Platforms (META [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/META]), Alphabet (GOOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOG]), and Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]) to impact the tech sector broadly. Stocks that have been jolted in the past during Nvidia's update include AMD (AMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMD]), Intel (INTC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTC]), Super Micro Computer (SMCI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SMCI]), and Broadcom (AVGO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVGO]). Options trading implies a 6% share price move for Nvidia after its earnings report drops.

THURSDAY - FEBRUARY 26

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Royal Bank of Canada (RY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RY]), Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TD]), Monster Beverage (MNST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MNST]), Intuit (INTU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTU]), Dell Technologies (DELL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DELL]), and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBD]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Duolingo (DUOL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DUOL]) and Figs (FIGS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FIGS]) after they report.
 	* All day - Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman is scheduled for Senate Banking Committee testimony. 
 	* 8:30 A.M. The January Durable Goods orders report will be released.

FRIDAY - FEBRUARY 27

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Pearson (PSO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSO]).
 	* 8:30 A.M. The January Producer Price Index report will be released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.
 	* 9:00 A.M. The U.S. Census Bureau will release the January Construction Spending report.
 	* ALL DAY - Jack in the Box (JACK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JACK]) will hold its annual shareholder meeting and director election, where the company is urging investors to back its full slate of 10 board nominees amid activist investor pushback.

MORE ON THE MARKETS 

 	* VT: Still The Best Approach To Passive Investing [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4872902-vt-still-the-best-approach-to-passive-investing]
 	* U.S. GDP Set To Bounce Back After Government Shutdown Depresses Q4 Activity [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4872938-us-gdp-set-to-bounce-back-after-government-shutdown-depresses-q4-activity]
 	* Why Static Portfolios Fail When Risk Regimes Change [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4872923-static-portfolios-fail-risk-regimes-change]
 	* Trump says shutdown cut “at least two points" from GDP ahead of fourth quarter read [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4554620-trump-says-shutdown-cut-at-least-two-points-in-gdp]
 	* SCOTUS ruling could put over $175B in U.S. tariff revenue at risk, Penn-Wharton estimates [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4554600-scotus-ruling-could-put-over-175b-in-us-tariff-revenue-at-risk-penn-wharton-estimates]

title: August 2025 UK Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership
content:
As the UK market grapples with the ripple effects of weak trade data from China, reflected in the recent downturn of the FTSE 100 and FTSE 250 indices, investors are keenly observing how global economic shifts influence domestic markets. In such volatile conditions, growth companies with high insider ownership can be appealing as they often demonstrate strong alignment between management and shareholder interests, potentially offering resilience amidst broader market fluctuations.

Top 10 Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In The United Kingdom

Below we spotlight a couple of our favorites from our exclusive screener.

Public Policy Holding Company

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Public Policy Holding Company, Inc. offers consulting services in the United States and has a market capitalization of £216.79 million.

Operations: The company's revenue is primarily derived from Government Relations at $102.46 million, followed by Public Affairs Consulting at $36.41 million and Diversified Services at $10.69 million.

Insider Ownership: 26.3%

Public Policy Holding Company is seeking to broaden its capital access through a U.S. IPO on Nasdaq, while maintaining its AIM listing. The company is trading significantly below estimated fair value and anticipates revenue growth of 11.2% annually, outpacing the UK market average. Profitability is expected within three years, although current dividends are not well covered by earnings. No substantial insider trading has been reported recently, indicating stable insider sentiment.

Operations: The company's revenue primarily comes from its oil and gas exploration and production segment, generating $1.31 billion.

Story Continues

Insider Ownership: 10.1%

Energean is poised for significant earnings growth, projected at 42.8% annually, surpassing the UK market average. Despite trading 33.4% below its estimated fair value, revenue growth is moderate at 10.8%. Recent operational halts due to geopolitical tensions have been resolved with production resuming safely. Insider activity shows more buying than selling recently, although substantial selling occurred over the past quarter. The dividend yield of 9.36% remains unsustainable as it's not well covered by earnings.

Overview: Integrated Diagnostics Holdings plc is a consumer healthcare company offering medical diagnostics services, with a market cap of $288.34 million.

Operations: The company's revenue primarily comes from its pathology services, generating EGP 4.49 billion, with an additional EGP 224 million from radiology services.

Insider Ownership: 27.9%

Integrated Diagnostics Holdings is expanding its radiology footprint in Egypt with the acquisition of CAIRO RAY for Radiotherapy, enhancing its service offerings and strategic positioning. The company's revenue growth is forecasted at 17.1% annually, outpacing the UK market average. Despite moderate expected earnings growth of 18.3%, IDH trades at a substantial discount to its estimated fair value, presenting potential value opportunities. High insider ownership aligns management interests with shareholders, though recent insider activity data is unavailable.

title: Pineapple Power Corporation to hold annual general meeting on July 28
content:
LONDON - Pineapple Power Corporation PLC, a special purpose acquisition company, announced Thursday that it has posted its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended December 31, 2024, along with the Notice of Annual General Meeting (AGM) to shareholders.

The AGM will take place at the offices of Fladgate LLP in London on Monday, July 28, 2025, at 11:00 am. According to the company’s statement, the meeting will focus solely on formal business matters without a presentation component.

The company stated that shareholders can submit questions related to the AGM via email until 11:00 am on Friday, July 25, 2025. The documents are also available on the company’s website.

Pineapple Power, which is listed on the  London Stock Exchange  (LON:LSEG), noted in its press release that it recognizes "the importance of continuing engagement with shareholders" and aims to provide opportunities for investors to raise questions with the board.

The company did not disclose specific agenda items for the upcoming meeting.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.
